full_text: Shadab Khan to Warner, Caught by Rizwan!! . The drinks break does what none of the Pakistani bowlers could, breaking Warner's concentration, as he goes chasing after a pretty innocuous looking delivery -- short, going across outside off with width. It bounces a touch extra though, kissing the top-half of his attempted punch through to the keeper. Well, well, well, and there's a clear gap between bat and all. But there was a noise. And Warner's been foxed into believing it to be the edge.

short_text: Shadab Khan to Smith, Caught by Fakhar Zaman!! Needless! But Pakistan won't mind. Smith just had to keep rotating strike
full_text: Shadab Khan to Smith, Caught by Fakhar Zaman!! Needless! But Pakistan won't mind. Smith just had to keep rotating strike and play second fiddle, but instead he attempts the big shot and perishes in the process. Credit to Shadab though as he teased Smith was a well flighted delivery, made him go for the slog sweep and that induced the top-edge, Zaman settles near deep mid-wicket and takes a good catch. A distraught Smith puts his head down, slams his bat onto the surface and walk off.

full_text: Shadab Khan to Mitchell Marsh, Caught by Asif Ali!! Shadab strikes second ball! The crowd find their voice again. This is a big wicket for Pakistan. These two are the in-form batters and they have managed to break this paratnership. Marsh did not want to allow Shadab to settle, he goes for the slog sweep, but it is never a good idea hitting against the turn, the top-edge skies straight up and Asif Ali runs in from long leg to settle under it. That was a tough catch but Pakistan have been on the money with their fielding today.

full_text: Shaheen Afridi to Mitchell Marsh, no run, Top, top decision from umpire Chris Gaffaney. He was under a lot of pressure with almost everyone at the venue appealing, but he kept his calm and made the right decision. Marsh is welcomed by an inswinging yorker, he is late in bringing his bat down and is pinned on the boot, the impact seemed iffy and the ball was swinging as well, just clipping leg-stump

short_text: Shaheen Afridi to Finch, Lbw!! Dubai erupts! Afridi has pinged Finch plumb in front. This was so dead that the Australia
full_text: Shaheen Afridi to Finch, Lbw!! Dubai erupts! Afridi has pinged Finch plumb in front. This was so dead that the Australian skipper does not even bother to discuss about the review, puts his bat under his arm and starts walking off. The inswinger and Finch is caught moving his front foot forward, the ball strikes him below the kneeroll and the impact was right in front of the stumps. No hesitation from umpire Chris Gaffaney as he raised the finger up as soon as Afridi appealed.
